rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/2021 1) The court will not approve the account until receipts are filed showing the estate was paid back Merrill Lynch's management fees in excess of the court ordered 0.75%. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENT CHI KIN SUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if defective notice should be waived. No original bank statement or letter from the bank has been filed to verify the cash alleged to be on hand, which is purportedly exactly the same amount as when the account was opened in 2017. The "detailed" schedule of receipts is not detailed; the source of the rental income is not disclosed. The liabilities schedule does not appear to be complete and the administrator may not be able to substantiate her allegation that she advanced personal funds. The Court might wish to see the escrow statement showing the costs associated with the loan. The attorney fee declaration appears to show services provided for Elaine Tan, individually, not for the benefit of the estate. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MANUEL AND LUCY ABEYTA FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Shirley Basch As Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Still no courtesy copy of the Receipt filed on 8/23/21 has been provided. It appears (also from the copy of the bank statement attached to the petitioner's second amended petition) that Olga Ortega sold the property as the successor trustee of the Survivor's Trust and deposited all of the proceeds into an account or accounts titled in the Lucy Abeyta Survivor's Trust. She was appointed as the successor trustee of the Manuel and Lucy Abeyta Family Trust on 12/21/20, not of the Survivor's Trust. 2. No ex parte petition has been filed yet to withdraw funds from that blocked account in order to make sure they are adequately maintained in FDIC-insured accounts. 3. Exhibit B to the second amended petition filed on 7/28/21 (and 8/13/21?) is incomplete; the net proceeds were cut off at the bottom of the copy of the closing statement. 4. It is unclear how the blocked accounts would be unblocked without a further Court order after certain conditions are met, as the petitioner proposes. 5. Counsel for the former trustee and now the successor trustee stated at the hearing on 9/27/21 that an Inventory and Appraisal would be provided, but none has been filed or, apparently, served on the petitioner and her attorney. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL BRAUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The declaration filed in response to the examiner's notes was only signed by the proposed executor's attorney. The declaration contains information that may or may not be particularly within the attorney's personal knowledge. CRC 7.104(b)(1). No supplement verified by the proposed executor has been filed to corroborate the attorney's declaration. CRC 7.103(c). 2) The competing petition for letters of administration has not been dismissed. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OLLIE BERNARD FOSTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) All the executors named in the decedent's will are deceased. The sole beneficiary of the decedent's estate is his spouse who survived the decedent, but is now deceased. Probate Code section 8441(b) provides in relevant part that: "A person who takes more than 50 percent of the value of the estate under the will has priority over other persons who take under the will". The petitioners are the personal representatives of the estate of the decedent's surviving spouse, the sole beneficiary under the decedent's will. 2) Co-petitioner Anastasia McDill appears to be the same person as "Jean" Anastasia McDill to whom letters were issued as co-personal representative of the estate of the decedent's spouse. The petition has not been amended to add "Jean" to her name in this matter. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTHONY B. BOWLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. issues are no notice to the decedent's daughter and unknown assets. Counsel may be asked to address whether they have ocnfirmed how title is held to the real properties. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE OSTBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/10/22. No earlier availability. No courtesy copies of the petition, proof of service, and Duties and Liabilities were received by the assigned Examiner as of 11/17/21 (as noted in the preliminary tentative ruling posted on 11/15/21). [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] (Only the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number and a proposed order have been received.) Courtesy copies of the petition and other papers and the proof of publication must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/29/21. A quick skim of the petition online (as a one-time courtesy) raised the following issues: When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] Neither the petition nor other E-filed papers for which no courtesy copy was provided have been reviewed yet in detail.
